what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment that points to an image asset in the design-assets channel.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
It declares an attachment id att-61dc2a14.
The target uses an image:// URI pointing to design-assets/icons/Icon_Gold_Lighting.
An ISO 8601 added timestamp is present.
standouts: Target image asset is Icon_Gold_Lighting in the design-assets/icons path.
Attachment is categorized under the design-assets channel.
The record is purely metadata with no embedded image data.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
Identifier follows an att- prefix pattern.
Target uses a custom image:// URI scheme.
Timestamp is in ISO 8601 format with timezone.
concerns: The image:// URI cannot be resolved or validated from the file alone.
No information about image format, dimensions, or file size.
No uploader or provenance metadata is recorded.
notes: A sparse metadata stub that registers a design-assets image attachment named Icon_Gold_Lighting.
questions: What does the image:// URI scheme resolve to in storage?
What is the format, size, and dimensions of Icon_Gold_Lighting?
Who or what process added this attachment?
How does channel-memory relate to other channels or messages?
purpose_vs_actual: The file acts as an attachment record, which matches its stated metadata purpose.
intention_signals: It appears intended to register and locate a specific image asset within a design-assets channel so other tooling can reference it by id and URI.
novelty: The custom image:// URI scheme for targeting assets is the most distinctive element; otherwise the record is conventional.
contribution: Provides a machine-readable attachment record that links a stable identifier to an image asset location and its channel context.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment record in the design-assets channel linking id att-61dc2a14 to the image asset image://design-assets/icons/Icon_Gold_Lighting with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to register an image attachment so it can be referenced by id and URI within the design-assets channel context.
